name: patient-monitoring-block
title: Patient Monitoring Block
description: The deterioration surface — the flowsheet holds the record, the trend chart holds the shape, the early-warning score holds the trajectory.

props.name: flowsheet
props.description: The record: what was measured, when, and what was not.

props.name: trends
props.description: The shape: the same observations over time.

props.name: score
props.description: The trajectory: an aggregate the consumer's validated logic computed.

props.name: medications
props.description: Optional. Pass the same `times` as the trends and the two align.

props.name: freshness
props.description: Sits in the header — it qualifies everything below it.

clinicalNotes: All three surfaces are present because none of them is sufficient alone. A flowsheet shows every value and no trend. A chart shows the trend and hides what was never measured. An aggregate score compresses both into a number a deteriorating patient can sit inside for hours. Read together, a gap in the grid, a slope in the chart, and a climbing score are one story — and the demo is built to show exactly that: a missed 18:00 round, a rising NEWS2, and three beta-blocker doses held for a soft blood pressure. The freshness stamp is in the header rather than beside a panel because it qualifies the whole surface: none of this is current if the monitor stopped reporting at six. The block displays; it escalates nothing and computes no score.
notes: Layer-M4 block. Client component. Composes vitals-flowsheet + vitals-trend-chart + early-warning-score + medication-timeline + data-freshness-stamp.
